questions.question: Identify the figure given in the options which when put in the place of the question mark (?) will logically complete the series.
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: · The positions of W and V are interchanged. Then the positions of remaining elements are shifted forward. P, R and X are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. Also, G, H and F are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. · The positions of F and X are interchanged. Then the positions of remaining elements are shifted forward. P, R and V are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. Also, G, H and W are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. · The positions of W and V are interchanged. Then the positions of remaining elements are shifted forward. P, R and F are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. Also, G, H and X are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. · The positions of F and X are interchanged. Then the positions of remaining elements are shifted forward. P, R and W are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. Also, G, H and V are shifted forward in the clockwise direction. Thus, option figure (I) will replace the question mark in the given series. Option figure II is incorrect because the positions of V and H and W and P are interchanged. Option figure III is incorrect because the position of R and H are interchanged and Q is added instead of H. Option figure IV is incorrect because the positions of G, H, V and X are not correct.

questions.question: A paper is folded and cut as shown below. How will it appear when unfolded?
questions.explanation: Thus, option figure (IV) is the correct answer. So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A cube is made by folding the given sheet. Which of the following alphabet will appear at the top side, if 'A' appears at the left side and 'B' appears at the front side of the cube?
questions.explanation: In the given format, A lies opposite to P, C lies opposite to M, B lies opposite to Z. So, if A appears at the left side, then P appears at the right side. If B appears at the front side, then Z appears at the back side. Therefore, the remaining alphabets are C and M. So, either C or M appears at the top side.

questions.question: '1 ₰ 2' means '1 is the daughter of 2' '1 ≈ 2' means '1 is the son of 2' '1 ∂ 2' means '1 is the brother of 2' '1 ∆ 2' means '1 is the wife of 2' As per which of the following expressions 'Z is the grandfather of U'?
questions.explanation: Option (a): W ∂ U ₰ Y ≈ Z W ∂ U means 'W is the brother of U' U ₰ Y means 'U is the daughter of Y' Y ≈ Z means 'Y is the son of Z' Z is either the grandfather or grandmother of U. Option (b): U ₰ W ≈ X ∆ Z U ₰ W means 'U is the daughter of W' W ≈ X means 'W is the son of X' X ∆ Z means 'X is the wife of Z' Z is the grandfather of U. Option (c): Z ∆ T ∂ W ∆ U Z ∆ T means 'Z is the wife of T' T ∂ W means 'T is the brother of W' W ∆ U means 'W is the wife of U' Z is the sister-in-law of U. Option (d): U ₰ V ∆ X ≈ Z U ₰ V means 'U is the daughter of V' V ∆ X means 'V is the wife of X' X ≈ Z means 'X is the son of Z' Z is either the grandfather or grandmother of U. Thus, the answer is option (b).

questions.question: 24 is related to 600 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 18 is related to 342. Which of the following numbers is related to 30 using the same logic?
questions.explanation: 24 + 242 = 600 18 + 182 = 342 Similarly, 30 + 302 = 930 Thus, the answer is option (b).

(The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letter/number of consonants/vowels in the word) Tyndall Effect : Scattering of Light :: Twinkling of Stars : ?
questions.explanation: Tyndall Effect is due to Scattering of Light. In the same way, Twinkling of Stars is due to Refraction of light.

questions.explanation: Manageable is the odd one out except for this the rest all are the synonyms of Hard.

questions.question: Which of the following interchanges of numbers (not digits) would make the given equation correct? 1281 ÷ 6 - 5 x 3 + 7 x 384 ÷ 14 x 2 - 17 + 9 = 36
questions.explanation: Option (a) If the numbers 6 and 9 are interchanged, then the equation will be: 1281 ÷ 9 - 5 x 3 + 7 x 384 ÷ 14 x 2 - 17 + 6 = 129 - 5 x 3 + 7 x 36 x 2 - 17 + 6 = 129 - 15 + 7 x 312 - 17 + 6 = 1216 - 15 x 318 - 17 = 121 x 31 = 36 So, the equation is correct. Option (b) If the numbers 2 and 3 are interchanged, then the equation will be: 1281 ÷ 6 - 5 x 2 + 7 x 384 ÷ 14 x 3 - 17 + 9 = 1213.5 - 5 x 2 + 7 x 36 x 3 - 17 + 9 = 1213.5 - 10 + 7 x 318 - 17 + 9 = 1220.5 - 10 x 327 - 17 = 1210.5 x 310 So, 1210.5 x 310 ≠ 36. So, the equation is incorrect. Option (c) If the numbers 7 and 17 are interchanged, then the equation will be: 1281 ÷ 6 - 5 x 3 + 17 x 384 ÷ 14 x 2 - 7 + 9 = 1213.5 - 5 x 3 + 17 x 36 x 2 - 7 + 9 = 1213.5 - 15 + 17 x 312 - 7 + 9 = 1230.5 - 15 x 321 - 7 = 1215.5 x 314 So, 1215.5 x 314≠ 36. So, the equation is incorrect. Option (d) If the numbers 3 and 14 are interchanged, then the equation will be: 1281 ÷ 6 - 5 x 14 + 7 x 384 ÷ 3 x 2 - 17 + 9 = 1213.5 - 5 x 14 + 7 x 328 x 2 - 17 + 9 = 1213.5 - 70 + 7 x 356 - 17 + 9 = 1220.5 - 70 x 365 - 17 = 12-49.5 x 348 So, 12-49.5 x 348 ≠ 36. So, the equation is incorrect.

questions.question: Select the combination of numbers so that the jumbled letters arranged accordingly will form a meaningful English word: G J W I A S 1 2 3 4 5 6
questions.explanation: Option (a): 4, 1, 6, 2, 5, 3 416253 IGSJAW IGSJAW, which is not a meaningful word. So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b): 3, 6, 2, 5, 4, 1 362541 WSJAIG WSJAIG, which is not a meaningful word. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c): 2, 4, 1, 6, 5, 3 241653 JIGSAW JIGSAW, which is a meaningful word. Jigsaw means a picture on cardboard/wood that is cut into small pieces and has to be fitted together again. So, option (c) is correct. Option (d): 4, 5, 2, 1, 3, 6 452136 IAJGWS IAJGWS, which is not a meaningful word. So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: If today is Thursday, then which day is it exactly 36 th day after today?
questions.explanation: Today is Thursday. 36 th day = 3*3*3*3*3*3 = 729 If we divide 729 by 7, then the remainder will be 1 So, 36 th day after today = Thursday + 1 = Friday.

questions.question: Which of the given options would be a meaningful ascending order of the following words? I: Dawn II: Dusk III: Night IV: Morning V: Sunrise
questions.explanation: Dawn - the period in the day when light from the sun begins to appear in the sky Dusk - the time before night when it is not yet dark Night - the part of every 24-hour period when it is dark because there is very little light from the sun Morning - the part of the day from the time when the sun rises or you wake up until the middle of the day or lunch time Sunrise - the time in the morning when the sun starts to rise in the sky So, the correct order is: I, V, IV, II, III Thus, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third letter cluster in the same way as the second letter cluster is related to the first letter cluster and the sixth letter cluster is related to the fifth letter cluster. DNT: BFLPRV :: IMV : ? :: PGX : NREIVZ

Three statements are given followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All Books are paper No paper is Novel All Notes are Novel Conclusions: I: No Book is Notes II: No Paper is Notes III: Some Notes may be Books
options: Both conclusion I and II follows
questions.explanation: Venn diagram: Conclusions: I: No Book is Notes ----- True as no paper is novel ---- So Follow II: No Paper is Notes ----- True as no paper is novel ---- So Follow III: Some Notes may be Books ---- Can't say as it can be possible ---- So Does not follow So, both conclusion I and II follows. Thus, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The sentence below has a word in which the letters are jumbled up. Rearrange the letters of that word, written in capital letters, to form the correct word. I think the Home Office should seriously reconsider his urgent APEL for asylum forthwith.
questions.explanation: After jumbling of letters, the correct word for the given sentence is PLEA. I think the Home Office should seriously reconsider his urgent PLEA for asylum forthwith.

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark (?) in it. 11 (359) 8; 7 (254) 9; 20 (527) ?

'1 ! 2' means '2 is the sister of 1'. '1 + 2' means '2 is the father of 1'. '1 = 2' means '2 is the daughter-in-law of 1'. '1 & 2' means '2 is the child of 1'. If T & U ! E = D + J, then how is D's husband related to T?
questions.explanation: It is given that: '1 ! 2' means '2 is the sister of 1'. '1 + 2' means '2 is the father of 1'. '1 = 2' means '2 is the daughter-in-law of 1'. '1 & 2' means '2 is the child of 1'. The given expression is: T & U ! E = D + J After decoding we get: U is the child of T. E is the sister of U. D is the daughter-in-law of E. J is the father of D. So, D's husband is the grandson of T.

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the following number series? 1103, 982, ?, 801, 737
questions.explanation: The given series follows - 112, - 102, - 92, - 82 .. pattern. Thus, the series becomes 1103, 982, 882, 801, 737. Hence, 882 will replace the question mark. Hence, option b is correct.

questions.explanation: We have: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 F + 2 = H, H+ 2= J , J+ 2 = L , L + 2 =N L+ 1 = M, M+1 = N, N+ 1= O, O+ 1= P The exponent increases by 2 in each step.

Consider the following statements: 1) India has notified 18 Biosphere Reserves under its national scheme, of which 12 are officially recognized under the UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR). 2) The Gulf of Mannar Biosphere Reserve, situated between India and Sri Lanka, is a significant marine ecosystem noted for its coral reefs and the presence of the vulnerable Dugong (Sea Cow). Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: India has notified 18 Biosphere Reserves under the national program. 12 of these have been included in the UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR). The most recent inclusion was the Panna Biosphere Reserve (2020). The Gulf of Mannar Biosphere Reserve lies between Tamil Nadu (India) and Sri Lanka. It has over 4,000+ species, including corals, seagrasses, mangroves, and many marine organisms. It is one of the few places in India where the Dugong (sea cow) is found - a vulnerable species. Further insights Biosphere Reserves have a three-zone system: Core, Buffer, and Transition, ensuring both conservation and sustainable use. They integrate biodiversity conservation with community participation, especially involving local and tribal groups. Biosphere Reserves often contain multiple protected areas, such as national parks and wildlife sanctuaries within them.

questions.question: The Gross National Product (GNP) at market prices is equals to:
options: GDP at market price - Net Factor Income from Abroad
GDP at market price + Net Factor Income from Abroad
GNP at Market Price - Net Indirect Taxes
questions.explanation: The Gross National Product (GNP)is the monetary value of all the goods and services produced within the domestic territory of a country in an accounting year corrected for net income from abroad. While computing GNP trans-boundary activities of an economy is also taken into account. GNP = GDP + (Factor Income from abroad) - (Factor income abroad) GNP (at Market Price) = GDP (at Market Price) + Net Factor Income from Abroad

questions.question: In December 2025, a commemorative postal stamp was released to honour which great Emperor and patron of Tamil culture?
questions.explanation: A commemorative postal stamp was released in honour of Emperor PerumbiduguMutharaiyar II, also known as Suvaran Maran, who was celebrated as a great patron of Tamil culture and notable ancient ruler. The Government of India issued a special postage stamp to honour this emperor from early medieval Tamil history because of his significant contributions to Tamil culture and his legacy as a distinguished administrator Further Insights PerumbiduguMutharaiyar II, known as Suvaran Maran, ruled parts of central Tamil Nadu in the 7th-8th century CE. He belonged to the Mutharaiyar dynasty and was known for strong governance and cultural patronage. The stamp release event was presided over by the Vice-President of India, C. P. Radhakrishnan. The event highlighted efforts to honour unsung regional heroes in Indias history. The stamp release aligns with initiatives promoting Tamil culture and heritage nationally.

questions.question: The Treaty of Yandabo (1826), which resulted in the British East India Company acquiring territories like Assam and Manipur, was signed between the British and which of the following?
questions.explanation: After defeating Burma, the British forced the Kingdom of Ava to sign the treaty and give up Assam and Manipur. The Treaty of Yandabo was signed in 1826 after the First Anglo-Burmese War (1824 - 26). It was concluded between the British East India Company and the Kingdom of Ava (Burma). Under this treaty, Burma ceded Assam, Manipur, Arakan, and Tenasserim to the British and agreed to pay an indemnity. Further Insights The treaty marked the beginning of British expansion in Northeast India. Assam formally came under British control after 1826. The war proved costly for the Company in men and money. Burma became financially weakened due to heavy indemnity. The treaty reshaped the political map of Northeast India.

questions.question: As per the 91st Constitutional Amendment (2003), the total number of Ministers, including the Prime Minister, in the Union Council of Ministers shall not exceed ____ of the total strength of the Lok Sabha.
questions.explanation: The 91st Amendment inserted Article 75(1A) prescribing that the total number of Ministers (including the Prime Minister) shall not exceed "C. 15%" of the total number of members of the House of the People (Lok Sabha). - An analogous cap applies to states (Article 164(1A)), with a minimum of 12 ministers. - The amendment also introduced anti-defection reinforcements, including restrictions on office of profit and disqualification for defectors. Incorrect options: - "A. 10%": No such cap at the Union level in the Constitution. - "B. 12%": Applies historically to size of Council of Ministers in some conventions, not constitutionally mandated at Union level. - "D. 1/6th": Not a constitutional cap for Council of Ministers. Further Insights: - 91st Amendment also limits appointment of non-legislators as ministers to a maximum of six months before they must secure membership. - The anti-defection law is in the Tenth Schedule; disqualified members cannot be appointed as ministers until re-elected. - Cabinet Committees are set up by executive order; their number/portfolios can change over time (all chaired by PM except where specified otherwise).

questions.question: With reference to the '9th Siddha Day' celebrated in January 2026, consider the following statements: i) The theme for the 2026 celebration was "Siddha for Global Health." ii) The day is observed annually to commemorate the birth anniversary of Sage Agathiyar. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement i and ii both are correct The 9th Siddha Day in January 2026 was celebrated with the theme Siddha for Global Health. This theme highlighted the relevance of Siddha medicine in promoting preventive and holistic health at a global level. Siddha Day is observed annually on 6 January to commemorate the birth anniversary of Sage Agathiyar, who is revered as the father of the Siddha system of medicine. Further Insights Sage Agathiyar is one of the earliest Siddhars and is traditionally credited with systematizing Siddha medical knowledge in Tamil culture. Siddha is one of Indias oldest traditional medical systems, with deep roots in Tamil Nadus history. The Ministry of AYUSH promotes Siddha Day as part of efforts to integrate traditional medicine into modern healthcare frameworks. The 2026 Siddha Day celebrations were held at KalaivanarArangam, Chennai with participation from practitioners, researchers, and students. Siddha medicine emphasizes a holistic, preventive and wellness-oriented approach to health and disease.

questions.question: According to 2011 Census in India, which of the following is the best performing state in respect of sex ratio in the age group of 0 to 6 years?
questions.explanation: The Best performing states in respect of Sex Ratio in the age group of 0 to 6 years in census 2011 were Chhattisgarh (969 Females), Kerala (964 Females), Assam (962 Females), West Bengal (956 Females) Jharkhand (948 Females), and Karnataka (948 Females).

questions.question: In Hindustani classical music, the most common way to classify a raga is under _____ parent scales called thaat.
questions.explanation: In Hindustani classical music, the most widely accepted system for classifying ragas is the Thaat system, which groups them under 10 parent scales. This system was propagated by the eminent musicologist Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande in the early 20th century. Each thaat is a heptatonic scale (containing seven notes) from which a number of ragas can be generated. Further insights: The 10 thaats as defined by Bhatkhande are: Bilawal, Kalyan, Khamaj, Bhairav, Poorvi, Marwa, Kafi, Asavari, Bhairavi, and Todi. While the Thaat system is a useful pedagogical tool, it's not a rigid rule, and some ragas may exhibit characteristics of more than one thaat. It is a system of classification, not a system of composition. It was a Carnatic musicologist - Venkatmukhi of the 17th century, who gave a system of 72 melas formed out of these twelve tones. Later on, in the 20th century, Pt. Bhatkhande, chose 10 out of the 72 to classify Hindustani ragas. Thus in Hindustani (North Indian) classical music, the most common way to classify a raga is under ten parent scales (called thaat).

questions.question: Which of the following rivers while in Tibet carry a smaller volume of water and less silt as it is a cold and a dry area and passes through a region of high rainfall in India?
questions.explanation: In Tibet, the Brahmaputra river carries a smaller volume of water and less silt as it is a cold and a dry area. In India, it passes through a region of high rainfall. Here the river carries a large volume of water and considerable amount of silt. The Brahmaputra has a braided channel in its entire length in Assam and forms many riverine islands. On its left bank, rivers like the Lohit, Dibang, Subansiri, Dhansiri, and Kopili contribute significantly to its flow. The right bank witnesses the confluence of rivers such as the Kameng, Manas, and Teesta, further enriching the river's volume. Origins of other rivers: Ganga: Originates from the Gangotri Glacier in the Uttarakhand Himalayas. Yamuna: Rises from the Yamunotri Glacier, also in the Uttarakhand Himalayas. Indus: Has its source in Tibet, near Lake Mansarovar. It flows west through the Himalayas and into Pakistan before emptying into the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Match the following column on the basis of the "Different layer of atmosphere and their extension in the atmosphere". List IList II (i) Stratosphere(a) Up to height of 50 km (ii) Troposphere(b) Up average height of 13 km (iii) Mesosphere(c) Between height of 80 km and 400 km (iv) Ionosphere(d) Up to height 80 km
questions.explanation: TroposphereIts average height is 13 km and extends roughly to a height of 8 km near the poles and about 18 km at the equator. StratosphereThe stratosphere is found above the tropopause and extends up to a height of 50 km. MesosphereThe mesosphere lies above the stratosphere, which extends up to a height of 80 km. IonosphereThe ionosphere is located between 80 and 400 km above the mesopause. It contains electrically charged particles known as ions, and hence, it is known as ionosphere.

questions.question: A person 1.80 m tall stands in front of a plane mirror. What is the minimum length of the mirror required for the person to see his full height?
questions.explanation: In a plane mirror, the minimum mirror length needed is half the person's height, independent of distance. 0.5 1.80 m = 0.90 m. Further Insights: - Image in a plane mirror is virtual, upright, and same size; image distance equals object distance. - Lateral inversion occurs in plane mirrors. - If the mirror rotates by θ, the reflected ray rotates by 2θ (useful for optical instruments). - Periscope uses multiple plane mirrors to raise the line of sight.

questions.question: With reference to India's long-term space roadmap, consider the following statements and identify the one that is incorrectly stated:
options: The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's indigenous capability for human spaceflight to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
India aims to establish the Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) by the year 2030.
The mission roadmap includes sending the first Indian astronaut to the Moon by the year 2040.
Gaganyaan serves as the foundation for India's transition toward a sustained human presence in space.
questions.explanation: The official goal for the Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) is 2035.not 2030 India plans to have its own independent space station fully operational by 2035. Further Insights Gaganyaan will place astronauts in Low Earth Orbit (~400 km). The Indian space station will be modular, similar in concept to the ISS. Gaganyaan involves human-rating of LVM3 rocket. ISRO's roadmap aligns with India's Amrit Kaal (2047) vision. Human spaceflight requires advances in life support, crew safety, and re-entry technology.

questions.question: What was the official theme for National Consumer Day observed on 24 December 2025?
options: Digital Markets: Making them Fairer for Consumers
Efficient and Speedy Disposal through Digital Justice
Consumer Protection in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
questions.explanation: The official theme for National Consumer Day 2025 was "Efficient and Speedy Disposal through Digital Justice." This theme highlights the government's focus on using digital tools and technology (such as online grievance redressal platforms and virtual hearings) to make consumer dispute resolution faster, accessible, and more efficient. Further Insights National Consumer Day in India commemorates the Presidents assent to the Consumer Protection Act, 1986 on 24 December. The Consumer Protection Act, 2019 provides enhanced frameworks for modern consumer dispute resolution mechanisms. Digital platforms like e-Jagriti and the National Consumer Helpline 2.0 have been key to improving consumer grievance redressal. Virtual hearings through digital justice platforms have increased accessibility for consumers nationwide.

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion(A) and Reason(R). Assertion (A): Most enzymes are proteins, but some RNAs also act as enzymes (ribozymes). Reason (R): In ribosomes, peptide bond formation is catalyzed by rRNA, not by a protein side chain.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is True. The majority of enzymes are indeed proteins, but catalytic RNAscalled ribozymesalso exist. They can catalyze biochemical reactions such as self-splicing and peptide bond formation. Reason (R) is True. The ribosomal large subunit RNA (23S rRNA in prokaryotes / 28S in eukaryotes) carries out the peptidyl transferase activity. The example given in R (catalytic role of rRNA in peptide bond formation) is direct evidence supporting A, because it demonstrates how RNA can function as an enzyme. Further Insights: - Thomas Cech and Sidney Altman shared the 1989 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for the discovery of catalytic RNA. - RNase P is an RNA-based enzyme that processes tRNA precursors. - The peptidyl transferase center is in the 23S (prokaryote) or 28S (eukaryote) rRNA of the large ribosomal subunit. - Enzyme catalysis often requires precise 3D structures-proteins and some RNAs achieve catalysis by specific folding.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the core theme of The Inheritance of Loss by Kiran Desai?
options: A magical-realist depiction of rural life in Odisha where time-traveling characters examine caste conflict
A postcolonial narrative set in the Himalayas exploring migration, identity, and globalization through multiple marginalized characters
A historical romance novel about British East India Company officers settling in Assam's tea gardens
A collection of semi-autobiographical essays portraying feminist struggles of immigrant women in America
questions.explanation: The Inheritance of Loss is set in the Himalayas with major themes of migration, living between two worlds, and between past and present, centering around the lives of Biju (an Indian living illegally in the United States) and Sai (an orphan living in mountainous Kalimpong with her grandfather). The major theme running throughout The Inheritance of Loss is one closely related to colonialism and the effects of post-colonialism: the loss of identity and the way it travels through generations. The novel explores immigration, identity, and relationships on both the interpersonal and international scale, spanning India, England, and the United States, detailing the conflict between traditional Indian ways of life and the shiny opulence of Western nations. Further insights The Inheritance of Loss is the second novel by Indian author Kiran Desai. It was first published in 2006. It won several awards, including the Booker Prize for that year, the National Book Critics Circle Fiction Award in 2007, and the 2006 Vodafone Crossword Book Award.

questions.question: Who is the central presiding deity honored during the famous 'Madai Festival' of the Bastar region in Chhattisgarh?
questions.explanation: The Madai Festival is a major tribal festival of the Bastar regionin Chhattisgarh. It is celebrated in honor of Danteshwari Temple's presiding deity. Goddess Danteshwari is considered the Kuladevi (clan goddess) of the Bastar rulers and tribal communities. Further Insights: Bastar region is known for its rich tribal culture and festivals. Goddess Danteshwari is regarded as an incarnation of Shakti. Madai Festival involves folk dances and traditional music. It is celebrated between December and March in different villages.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding PM-KUSUM: I. It aims to promote decentralized solar power through installation of solar pumps and solar plants. II. It provides 100% capital subsidy to farmers for installation of solar irrigation pumps. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is CORRECT. PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan) Scheme is aimed at ensuring energy security for farmers in India, with Component-A for Setting up of 10,000 MW of Decentralized Grid Connected Renewable Energy Power Plants on barren land. The scheme aims to promote decentralized renewable energy to agriculture sector by setting up solar power plants and solarizing agricultural pumps. The scheme has three main components: Component-A: Decentralized solar power plants Component-B: Standalone solar agriculture pumps Component-C: Solarization of grid-connected agriculture pumps Statement 2 is INCORRECT. The scheme does NOT provide 100% subsidy. Under the scheme, Central Financial Assistance (CFA) upto 30% of the Benchmark cost of the standalone solar pump will be provided. The State Government will give a subsidy of 30%; and the remaining 40% will be provided by the farmer.

questions.question: In February 2026, Devika Sihag entered the Thailand Masters women's singles final as world No. ______ in the BWF rankings.
questions.explanation: Young Indian shuttler, ranked world No.63, Devika Sihag clinched her maiden BWF Super 300 crown after Malaysia's Goh Jin Wei retired midway through the women's singles final of the Thailand Masters, a USD 250,000 event. The 20-year-old from Haryana was leading 21-8, 6-3 when world No. 68 Goh, a two-time former world junior champion, pulled out due to a hamstring injury, handing the Indian the biggest title of her career. Ranked world No. 63, Devika, who trains under coach Umendra Rana at the Padukone-Dravid Centre for Sports Excellence in Bengaluru, has also been sharpening her game alongside two-time Olympic medallist P. V. Sindhu under Indonesian coach Irwansyah Adi Pratama for the last few months.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Attorney General of India: a. He/she is appointed by the President under Article 76. b. He/she must be qualified to be appointed a Judge of the Supreme Court. c. He/she is entitled to speak and participate in Parliament, but cannot vote. d. He/she holds office at the pleasure of the President and has a fixed 5-year term. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement a is CORRECT. The President shall appoint a person who is qualified to be appointed a Judge of the Supreme Court to be Attorney-General for India. The Attorney General is appointed by the President of India at the instance of the Union Cabinet under Article 76(1) of the Constitution. Encyclopedia Britannica Statement b is CORRECT. He must be qualified to be appointed a judge of the Supreme Court. In other words, he must be a citizen of India and he must have been a judge of some high court for five years or an advocate of some high court for ten years or an eminent jurist, in the opinion of the President. Statement c is CORRECT. Every Minister and the Attorney-General of India shall have the right to speak in, and otherwise to take part in the proceedings of, either House, any joint sitting of the Houses, and any committee of Parliament of which he may be named a member, but shall not by virtue of this article be entitled to vote. This right is provided under Article 88 of the Constitution. Statement d is INCORRECT. While the first part is correct, the second part is false. The term of office of the Attorney General is not fixed by the Constitution. He holds the office during the pleasure of the President. There is no fixed term for the Attorney General of India. The Constitution mentions no specified tenure of the Attorney General. There is NO fixed 5-year term.

questions.question: In the context of the sport of Fencing, what does the term "Piste" refer to?
options: The protective mesh mask worn by the fencers to guard the face.
The linear playing area or strip on which a fencing bout takes place.
The specific point awarded for a successful touch on the opponent's torso.
The warning signal given by the referee when a fencer is being passive.
questions.explanation: In Fencing, the Piste is the field of play. It is a narrow, rectangular strip, usually about 14 meters long and 1.5 to 2 meters wide. Fencers must remain within the boundaries of the piste; stepping off the sides or the back result in penalties or the awarding of points to the opponent. Further Insights: The Strip: The piste is often made of metal, metallic mesh, or conductive material so that the electronic scoring system can distinguish between a hit on the opponent and a hit on the floor. En Garde Lines: These are the lines marked on the piste where fencers start the bout. Terminology for Equipment (Option a): The protective face gear is simply called a Mask, and the white protective jacket is a Plastron.

questions.question: Which of the following is a D block element?
questions.explanation: The d-block elements are found in groups 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12 of the periodic table; d-block elements are also known as the transition metals. The d orbital is filled with the electronic shell "n-1. " Na (Sodium), Mg (Magnesium), and Ca (Calcium) are all s-block elements because their valence electrons are in the s-orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following article of the Constitution lays down that the State shall take steps to organise village panchayats and endow them with such powers and, authority as may be necessary to enable them to function as units of self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40 of the Indian Constitution lays down that "The State shall take steps to organise village panchayats and endow them with such powers and authority as may be necessary to enable them to function as units of self-government." This article is part of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) under Part IV of the Constitution. It reflects the Gandhian principle of village self-governance and decentralization of power. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Article 15: This is a Fundamental Right (Part III) that prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth. (b) Article 25: This is a Fundamental Right (Part III) that guarantees freedom of conscience and free profession, practice, and propagation of religion. (c) Article 32: This is a Fundamental Right (Part III) that provides the right to constitutional remedies, empowering citizens to move the Supreme Court for the enforcement of their Fundamental Rights. Further insights: While Article 40 provided a directive, the constitutional status and framework for Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) were formally established through the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992. This amendment added Part IX ("The Panchayats") to the Constitution and the Eleventh Schedule, which lists 29 subjects within the purview of Panchayats. The 73rd Amendment mandated the establishment of a three-tier system of Panchayati Raj in states (village, intermediate, and district levels), regular elections, reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and women, and the constitution of State Finance Commissions and State Election Commissions for Panchayats. Article 40 thus served as an important inspiration and guiding principle for the subsequent constitutional and legislative measures to strengthen local self-government in rural India.

questions.question: A type of court called 'Kantakasodhana' was prevalent in the ______ Empire.
questions.explanation: The 'Kantakasodhana' courts were a key feature of the judicial system of the Mauryan Empire. Information about these courts is found in Kautilya's Arthashastra, the ancient Indian treatise on statecraft. The Mauryan judicial system had two main branches: Dharmasthiya courts, which dealt with civil matters like disputes over property and contracts, and Kantakasodhana courts, which handled criminal cases and maintained law and order. The term 'Kantakasodhana' literally means "removal of thorns," referring to the elimination of anti-social elements. Further insights: The Kantakasodhana courts were presided over by three commissioners (pradeshtris) and were powerful institutions responsible for suppressing crime, protecting the state from threats, and ensuring the smooth functioning of the administration.

questions.question: Who was the Prime Minister of India when the New Economic Policy (NEP) was introduced in 1991?
questions.explanation: • P. V. Narasimha Rao was the Prime Minister who spearheaded the implementation of the NEP in 1991, alongside Finance Minister Dr. Manmohan Singh. Further Insight: • Economic Crisis of 1991: ○ India faced a severe financial crisis in 1991, marked by a Balance of Payments (BoP) crisis. ○ Reasons included a decline in exports, dwindling foreign currency reserves, and rising inflation. ○ India had to borrow $7 billion from the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD), part of the World Bank, and the International Monetary Fund (IMF) under the condition of implementing economic reforms.

questions.question: Which statement about the National Planning Committee (1938) of the Indian National Congress is correct?
options: It was proposed by Subhas Chandra Bose and chaired by Jawaharlal Nehru.
It was proposed by Jawaharlal Nehru and chaired by Subhas Chandra Bose.
It was proposed by M. K. Gandhi and chaired by Sardar Patel.
It was proposed and chaired by M. Visvesvaraya under the Bombay Presidency.
questions.explanation: Under Subhas Chandra Bose's INC presidency (Haripura, 1938), the National Planning Committee was constituted with Jawaharlal Nehru as Chairman to lay out a comprehensive strategy for industrialization and social justice. It produced reports that later influenced post-independence planning. Incorrect options: - "B": Nehru chaired the committee, but the initiative to set it up at the party level was Bose's. - "C": Gandhi was skeptical of centralized state planning; Sardar Patel did not chair the NPC. - "D": M. Visvesvaraya authored "Planned Economy for India" (1934); the later "Bombay Plan" (1944) was by industrialists, not the NPC. Further Insights: - The NPC advocated state control of key industries and heavy industrialization. - Its work fed into the creation of the Planning Commission in 1950, with Nehru as its first chairman. - The National Development Council, formed in 1952, coordinated planning between Centre and States.

questions.question: In how many months will Rs 14,000 yield Rs 4130.406 as compound interest at 36% per annum compounded quarterly?
For what value of k will be following system of equations have no solution? (k - 1)x + 9y + 3 = 0, 14x - 6y + 17 = 0
questions.explanation: Rearranging the equation: (k - 1)x + 9y = - 3 14x - 6y = - 17 For no solution: a1/a2 = b1/b2 ≠ c1/c2 (k - 1)/14 = 9/(-6) ≠ -3/-17 (k - 1)/14 = 9/(-6) k - 1 = - 21 k = -20

questions.question: 9(173 - 73)_________172 + 72 + x = 90, then find the value of x

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct? I: If principal = Rs. 17640, amount = Rs. 38808, and time = 8 years, then at simple interest rate of interest is 10%. II: If total amount = 16560, time = 6 years, and rate of interest = 14%, then principal is Rs. 9000 on simple interest.
questions.explanation: Statement I: Let the rate of interest be 10%. Then, [1 + (10*8)/100]% of principal should be equal to the amount. Now, 180% of 17640 = Rs.31752 which is not equal to Rs.38808. Hence, statement I is not correct. Statement II: Let Rs. 9000 be the principal. [1 + (14 * 6)/100]% of 9000 should be equal to Rs.16560. 184% of 9000 = Rs.16560 which is equal to given amount. Hence, statement II is correct.

questions.question: The profit earned when a vase is sold at Rs. 175 is 3_________4 times of the loss incurred when the same vase is sold at Rs. 126. What will be the selling price of the vase if 14 2_________7 % profit is earned?
questions.explanation: Let CP of the vase is Rs. P So, (175 - P) = 3/4 * (P - 126) => 700 - 4P = 3P - 378 => 7P = 1078 => P = Rs. 154 So, selling price when profit is 14(2/7)% = 8/7 * 154 = Rs. 176

questions.question: A circular cardboard of radius 7cm is cut from a semi-circular cardboard of radius 14cm. Find the area of the remaining cardboard.
questions.explanation: Radius of semi-circular cardboard R = 14 cm Radius of circular cardboard r = 7 cm Area of the remaining cardboard = Area of semi-circular cardboard - Area of circular cardboard = (πR2/2) - πr2 = = 22 * 7 = 154 cm2

questions.question: If 1 kg of color covers 32 square feet and the cost of color is Rs 50 per kg, then how much will it cost to color the outer-side of a cube having 16 feet each side?
questions.explanation: Surface are of cube = 6*a2 = 6*162 = 1536 sq. ft So, quantity of color = 1536/32 = 48 kg Therefore, cost of coloring = 48*50 = Rs 2400

questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 10 days and B can do it in 12 days. They work together for 3 days. Then B leaves and A alone continues. 2 days after that C joins and the work is completed in 2 days more. In how many days can C do it, if he works alone?
questions.explanation: According to the question 10 A = 12 B Efficiency ratio of A : B = 6 : 5 Total work = 10 × 6 = 60 Work done by A and B in 3 days = (6 + 5) × 3 = 33 A will work 4 more days Work done by A alone in 4 days = 4 × 6 = 24 Remaining work = 60 – 33 – 24 = 3 Let the efficiency of C be x, then 2x = 3 x = 3/2 C complete the whole work in = 60/(3/2) = 40 days."

questions.question: If a(a+2b) = 115, b(b+2c) = 209, c(c+2a) = 117, then what is the value of (a + b + c), if a, b, c >0?
Find the value of 4 sin2 θ + tan θ + 5 cot θ_________3 cosec2 θ - 2 tan θ , if θ = 45°
questions.explanation: On putting θ = 45° = 8/4 = 2

questions.question: In the given figure find ∠RPQ interms of θ?
questions.explanation: " Clearly "

questions.question: How much percentage (to the nearest integer) above the cost price does a seller mark his goods so that after allowing a discount of 30% percent, he should gain 15%?
questions.explanation: Marked price/Cost price = (100 + Profit%)/(100 - discount%) MP/CP = 115/70 MP/CP = 23/14 Required percentage = (9/14) * 100 = 64.28 = 64%

questions.question: How many cubes with an edge length of √7 cm can fit a cuboid with dimensions of length, breadth, and height given to be 4√7 m, 6√7 m, and 3√7 m, respectively?
questions.explanation: Required number of cubes = Volume of cuboid/volume of each cube

questions.question: A solid sphere is formed by cutting a bigger solid sphere into two equal hemispheres and then joining these two hemispheres, each having a total surface area of 115.5 m2. If the total surface area of the bigger sphere is 576π m2, find the thickness of the remaining solid figure.

A person wishes to make a 100 sq m rectangular garden. Since he has only 30 m barbed wire for fencing, he fences only three sides letting the house wall act as the fourth side. The width of the garden is:
questions.explanation: " Let width of the garden be x m x = 10 is ruled out, because in that case, the garden will become square which is contrary to what is given. The width of the garden = 5 m. "

questions.question: The centres of two circles are C and D and their radii are r cm and R cm. If C + D = r + R, then the number of common tangents between them is:
questions.explanation: Given, C + D = r + R Distance between centres = Sum of radii Therefore, circle will touch each other externally and, in this case, number of common tangents between them will be 3.

questions.question: A flag 20 m high is standing on the top of a building. An angle 'a' is subtended by the height of the flag at a distance of 70m from the foot of the building, where tan a = 1/6.Then what is the height of the building?
questions.explanation: We have drawn the figure as the information is provided in the question. QS is the flag which is standing over a building SR. And according to the question, tan a = 1/6 ..(1) And in the triangle PRS, tan b = h/70 ..(2) Now in triangle PQR, tan (a + b) = (20+h)/70 As we know that tan (a + b) = (tan a + tan b)/(1 - tan a.tan b) So, (20+h)/70 = (tan a + tan b)/(1 - tan a.tan b) Now in this expression we will put values from equation (1) and (2) And after solving we will get ⇒ h2 + 20h -3500 = 0 ⇒ (h + 70) (h - 50) = 0 As h can't be negative so h = 50m

questions.question: A triangle ABC is right angled at C. Then Tan A + Tan B is equal to
questions.explanation: In the given figure, tan A = a/b and tan B = b/a Hence, tan A + tan B = a/b + b/a = (a2 + b2)/ab = c2/ab

questions.question: If the lateral surface area of a square prism is 160 cm2 and the volume of the prism is 160 cm 3, then find the length of paper that can cover the total surface area of the prism, where the width of the paper is 4 cm.
questions.explanation: Let the length of the side of the square base be a cm And let the height of the prism be h cm According to the question, Lateral surface area = perimeter of base × height So, 4 × a × h = 160 cm 2 a × h = 40 ……..(i) Since, volume of prism = area of base × height So, a 2 × h = 160 cm 3 ……. (ii) On dividing (ii) from (i), we get a = 4 cm Now, from (i) 4 × h = 40 So, h = 10 cm So, the total surface area of prism = 2 × area of base + lateral surface area 2 × 4 2 + 4 × 4 × 10 = 192 cm 2 So, the required length of the paper = 192/4 = 48 cm

questions.question: In the following figure, WXYZ is a rectangle. Find the measure of ∠ MNY.
questions.explanation: Since ∠ WZY = 90° Therefore, ∠ MZY = ∠ WZY - ∠ WZM ∠ MZY = 90 - 40 = 50° In Δ MZN, ∠ MNY is an external angle. Therefore, ∠ MNY = ∠ NMZ + ∠ MZN = 94 + 50 = 144°

questions.question: Which of the following is correct set for 0.8, 0.8̅, 0.08̅, 0.008̅.
questions.explanation: 0.8 = 8/10 = 720/900, second 0.8̅ = 8/9 = 800/900, Highest 0.08̅ = 8/90 = 80/900, 3rd 0.008̅ = 8/900 = 8/900, smallest If denominators are same, then number with highest numerator will be greater.

questions.question: The table given below shows the distribution of workers in four different fast food chain companies. CompanyNumber of outletsAverage number of workers per outletMale: Female P5102: 3 Q851: 3 R4205: 3 S657: 3 The ratio of number of female workers in company R to the number of male workers in company Q is:
questions.explanation: Number of female workers in company R = 20 x 4 x (3/8) = 30 Number of male workers in company Q = 8 x 5 x (1/4) = 10 Required Ratio = 30: 10 = 3: 1

questions.question: The average monthly salary of all the employees in a factory is Rs 8840. If the average salary of all the officers is Rs 15000 and that of the remaining employees is Rs 8000, then what is the difference between total number of officers and employees ?
questions.explanation: " Using the allegation method: 15000 8000 8840 840 : 6160 Ratio of Officers to remaining employees = 840: 6160 Required difference = 6160 - 840 = 5320"

questions.question: A policeman spots a thief at a distance of X meters. As the thief starts running, the policeman begins chasing him. If the thief and the policeman run at speeds of 24 km/h and 25 km/h, respectively, and after 3 minutes, the distance between them is 3/20 km, what is the value of X?
questions.explanation: Relative speed of policeman with respect to thief = 25 - 24 = 1 km/h Distance covered by policeman and thief after 3 minutes = 1 x (3/60) km = 1/20 km Initial distance = X m Distance between them after 3 minutes = 3/20 km Therefore, X - (1/20) = (3/20) X = (3/20) + (1/20) X = 1/5 km or 200 m

questions.question: The value of X * (3tan230° + 3sin30° + 6tan230° + 5sin30° + 9tan230° + 7sin30° + ..... + 45 tan230°) is a perfect square of a positive number. Then find the value of X.

In the following question, a statement has been given with highlighted text. Select the option that can replace the text with correct idiom or phrase. It seems a lot of effort but I'm sure it's the best solution eventually.
questions.explanation: 'In the long run' means 'eventually, ultimately'. Hence, option (c) is the answer. 'On tenterhooks' means 'in a state of suspense and anxiety'. 'At my wit's end' means 'in a state where one does not know what to do'. 'By leaps and bounds' means 'rapidly'.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. They wanted someone responsible to ______ the place at night-time.
questions.explanation: held up: rob someone or something using threats or violence. jump at: accept an opportunity or offer with great eagerness. lay by: save money look after: take care of someone or something. 'Held' is the past/past participle form of the verb 'hold'. Here, 'to' is an infinitive marker which requires a base verb after it. So, we cannot use 'held' after the infinitive marker. Options (b) and (c) do not give the right meaning to the sentence. Hence, they are negated. The use of 'look after' is completely justified to talk about the 'protection' of the place. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Note: Infinitive marker: A word or affix attached to the stem of a verb in order to form the full infinitive. In English, to is the infinitive marker. Participle: A word formed from a verb (e.g. going, gone, being, been) and used as an adjective (e.g. working woman, burnt toast) or a noun (e.g. good breeding). In English participles are also used to make compound verb forms (e.g. is going, has been). Consider the sentences given below: Dancing at the party, the women fell on the ground. Encouraged by her parents, Rita went on to pursue her passion. The highlighted word in the first sentence is of the form Verb + ing and is used as a present participle. It is used to denote an action that is going on or incomplete or imperfect. The highlighted word in the second sentence is of the form verb + ed. It is used as a past participle. It denotes completed action or state of the thing spoken of. A past participle ends with the following: -ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n. One should take great care and never get confused between a gerund and present participle. They use the same forms but perform different functions: a gerund is a verbal noun, while a present participle is a verbal adjective.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. We decided to cancel the trip ________ the weather forecast predicted a storm.
questions.explanation: The sentence explains the reason behind cancelling the trip. The blank requires a conjunction showing cause or reason. The correct option is "because", which introduces the cause for the cancellation. The other options do not fit: "Unless" introduces a condition, not a reason. "Although" shows contrast, not cause. "Until" indicates time, not reason. Thus, the correct sentence is: "We decided to cancel the trip because the weather forecast predicted a storm." Hence, option (c) is the answer. Note: "CONJUNCTIONS A conjunction (also called a connective) is a word such as and, because, but, for, if, or, when etc. Conjunctions are used to connect phrases, clauses, and sentences."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'ancient' fits the blank appropriately because it describes something very old or from a long time ago, which matches the context of the Silk Road being an ancient network of trade routes. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Aggravated: worsened or made more severe. Agitated: disturbed or unsettled. Abusive: using or involving physical violence or emotional cruelty.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'exchange' fits the blank appropriately because it means the giving and receiving of something in return, which perfectly describes what happened along the Silk Road with the trading of goods. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. Hospitality: the friendly and generous reception and treatment of guests or visitors. Interaction: the communication or involvement between two or more entities. Patronage: the support or sponsorship given by a patron, typically to an artist, organization, or cause.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'diverse' fits the blank appropriately because it means having a variety of different types or forms, which accurately describes the different civilizations that interacted along the Silk Road. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. Intense: something that is very strong, extreme, or deep in degree or intensity. Precise: exact or accurate in measurement, time, or judgment. Dense: something that is closely compacted together or crowded.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'profoundly' fits the blank appropriately because it means deeply, which accurately describes the impact of these innovations on societies along the Silk Road. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Happily: in a happy way. Proudly: in a manner that shows pride or satisfaction. Greedily: in a way that shows a wish to have a lot more food, money, etc. than you need.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, 'shaped' fits the blank appropriately because it means to have a significant influence on the form or nature of something, which accurately describes what the Silk Road did to the economic, cultural, and intellectual landscapes across continents. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Ignored: to pay no attention to something or to disregard it. Watched: to observe or monitor something closely. Embraced: to accept or welcome something enthusiastically.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to a place where animals are butchered?
questions.explanation: 'Abattoir' is the appropriate one-word substitute for the given group of words. The term 'abattoir' describes a place where animals are slaughtered and butchered for meat. 'Sinecure' refers to a position or job that requires little or no work, but provides a salary or other benefits. 'Cemetery' refers to a place where dead people are buried. 'Burrow' refers to a hole or tunnel in the ground made by an animal, such as a rabbit. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate antonym of the word given in bold. Considering how badly they're paid and what little support they get, their dedication astounds me.
questions.explanation: Astounds - to surprise and shock someone, esp. with news of something that is completely unexpected. Bores - to talk or act in a way that makes someone lose interest. Both words have opposite meanings. So, "bores" is the required antonym. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Resumes - begin again or continue after a pause or interruption. Promotes - support or actively encourage (a cause, venture, etc.); further the progress of. Achieves - successfully bring about or reach (a desired objective or result) by effort, skill, or courage.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Eccentric
questions.explanation: The word 'eccentric' means unconventional and slightly strange. Hence, here, 'unconventional' is the correct choice. 'Swamp' is a seasonally flooded low land. 'Furore' is the outbreak of public anger or excitement. 'Timid' means 'lacking in self-assurance, courage, or bravery; easily alarmed; timorous; shy'. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct synonym of EXPEDITIOUS:
questions.explanation: Expeditious - done with speed and efficiency. Tedious - too long, slow, tiresome or monotonous; deliberate - careful and unhurried; leisurely - unhurried or relaxed; prompt - done without delay. Thus, 'prompt' is the only word that can be a synonym of the word 'expeditious'. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech. The scientist declared that by the next decade, her team would have been conducting groundbreaking research on that specific gene for over twenty years.
options: The scientist said, "By the next decade, my team would have been conducting groundbreaking research on this specific gene for over twenty years."
The scientist said, "By the next decade, my team will be conducting groundbreaking research on this specific gene for over twenty years."
The scientist said, "By the next decade, my team would be conducting groundbreaking research on this specific gene for over twenty years."
The scientist said, "By the next decade, my team will have been conducting groundbreaking research on this specific gene for over twenty years."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Perfect Continuous Conditional Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech should be in Future Perfect Continuous Tense. Perfect Continuous Conditional (Subject + would/should + have + been + V1 + ing + Object) should be converted into Future Perfect Continuous (Subject + will/shall + have + been + V1 + ing + Object). For example: The girl said that they would have been waiting there". (Indirect Speech) The girl said, "They will have been waiting here". (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: 'declared that' is changed to 'said,'. 'her team' is changed to 'my team'. 'would have been conducting' is changed to 'will have been conducting'. 'that' is changed to 'this'. Also, inverted commas ("...") are placed in direct speech. Therefore, the correct answer is: The scientist said, "By the next decade, my team will have been conducting groundbreaking research on this specific gene for over twenty years." Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Consumers will see onion prices hike this week. We will store some onions.
options: Onion prices hike will have been seen by consumers this week. Some onions will be stored by us.
Onion prices hike will be seen by consumers this week. Some onions will be stored by us.
Onion prices hike will have being seen by consumers this week. Some onions will be stored by us.
Onion prices hike will be seen by consumers this week. Some onions will have been stored by us.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of the verb ['be' here] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Future Indefinite. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in the Future Indefinite. The format of Active voice is: Subject + shall/will + V1 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + shall/will + be + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: He will complete the assignment. (Active Voice) The assignment will be completed by him. (Passive Voice) The options except (b) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Hence, the correct answer is option (b). Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Choose the option that completes the sentence using the correct spelling. _______ cultural differences promotes tolerance and opens doors to new ideas.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, the correctly spelt word is 'embracing' and it means 'accepting or supporting something enthusiastically'. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Make a killing
options: To earn a lot of money very easily.
To not like something that someone says or does.
To kill someone using a gun.
questions.explanation: The idiom 'make a killing' means to earn a lot of money very easily. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Nuno felting is a meticulous and hands-on process in which the initial step involves selecting the right combination of wool and fabric, considering factors such as texture, colour, and weight. B. The next phase of the process includes wetting the wool-fabric composite with soapy water and gently massaging the fibres which encourages the wool to interlock with the fabric through agitation, initiating the felting process. C. Once the necessary material required is chosen, artisans delicately place the wool on the fabric, ensuring an even distribution of fibres. D. The combination of moisture, friction, and pressure causes the wool fibres to migrate through the fabric, creating a strong and integrated surface.
questions.explanation: Sentence A comes first and introduces the topic of discussion, i.e., Nuno felting and discusses the first step in this process, which includes selecting the right kind of material. Sentence C comes next and discusses the next step once the necessary material is chosen. Sentence B comes next and discusses the next phase of the process. Sentence D comes next, explaining what the combination of moisture, friction, and pressure (all the steps discussed in previous sentences) leads to. So, the correct sequence is ACBD. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The judgment was reserved by the magistrate.
options: The magistrate has reserved the judgement.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the verb 'was' and the preposition 'by' is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The Passive voice in this question is in Past Indefinite. Thus, the Active voice will also be in Past Indefinite. In Passive voice, the format is: Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). Thus, the format of Active voice is: Subject + V2 + Object. For example: The Champions League was won by Real Madrid. (Passive Voice) Real Madrid won the Champions League. (Active Voice) The options except (d) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Despite of the multitude of challenges/ brought on by the pandemic, teachers/ quickly adapted to digital methods/ to teach their students.
options: Despite of the multitude of challenges
brought on by the pandemic, teachers
questions.explanation: 'Despite' means without taking any notice of or being influenced by; not prevented by; in spite of. The use of 'of' is superfluous here. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Identify the part containing a grammatical or structural error: The authors examine the strength of the association among much different causal factors and the health status of mother and child.
options: much different causal factors and the
health status of mother and child
questions.explanation: The error is in part c) of the sentence. Use of word 'much' (used for uncountable nouns) is incorrect and needs to be replaced by 'many' which is used for countable nouns as the sentence talks about different 'factors' (countable noun). Hence, the answer is option c).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can replace the bracketed segment in the given sentence. Constructing complex models was challenging, (or it expanded) their understanding of engineering principles.
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, the phrase "or it expanded" creates a coordination issue because the conjunction "or" does not effectively coordinate with the preceding clause. The sentence begins by stating that constructing complex models was challenging, setting the expectation for a subsequent clause that complements or contrasts this difficulty. However, "or it expanded" doesn't properly coordinate with the challenging nature of constructing complex models. The use of the conjunction 'or' typically implies presenting an alternative or a choice between two or more possibilities. In the context of the sentence, the use of 'or' presents a binary contrast or alternative between two possibilities: either the challenging nature of constructing complex models or the expansion of understanding. However, it doesn't effectively align or coordinate with the first part of the sentence, leading to a lack of coherence or connection between the two clauses. Option (a) introduces the conjunction "But," which effectively addresses the coordination issue. "But" functions to present a contrasting or opposing idea to what was previously stated. In this context, it contrasts the challenging nature of constructing complex models with the subsequent expansion in understanding. It emphasises an unexpected positive outcome despite the initial difficulty. This usage of "But" helps to maintain the coherence of the sentence by appropriately contrasting the initial challenge with the subsequent improvement. Option (b) introduces "Because," implying a cause-and-effect relationship, suggesting that the expansion in understanding occurred due to the challenge of constructing complex models. However, this doesn't effectively capture the intended contrast between the difficulty of the task and the subsequent expansion in understanding. Option (c) does not rectify the original error (it continues to use the wrong conjunction 'or'). Option (d) is incorrect. The phrase "as well" typically functions to indicate an addition or inclusion of something in addition to what has already been mentioned. However, in the context provided, this phrase doesn't effectively establish a clear connection or logical progression between the challenging nature of constructing complex models and the subsequent expansion of understanding. It doesn't effectively align with the initial clause about the challenging nature of constructing complex models. It doesn't create the expected contrast or coherence needed in the sentence structure. The correct sentence is: Constructing complex models was challenging, but it improved their understanding of engineering principles. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. NOTE: CONJUNCTIONS A conjunction (also called a connective) is a word such as and, because, but, for, if, or, when etc. Conjunctions are used to connect phrases, clauses, and sentences.

questions.question: Identify the word that doesn't fit the part of speech needed. Her consistent hard work is a clear demonstrate of her dedication to the project.
questions.explanation: The word that doesn't fit the part of speech needed is c) demonstrate. The sentence uses the phrase "is a clear ______ of her dedication." This structure requires a noun after "clear" [adjective] to be modified by it and tell what "her consistent hard work" is. "Demonstrate" is a verb, meaning to clearly show the existence or truth of something by giving proof or evidence. The noun form is demonstration. The correct sentence would be: "Her consistent hard work is a clear demonstration of her dedication to the project." a): consistent: This is an adjective, correctly modifying "hard work." b): work: This is a noun (in this context, referring to effort), correctly used as part of "hard work." d): dedication: This is a noun, correctly used to refer to a quality.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The city had to hire extra trucks to collect the mounting refuse.
options: He was instructed to take the refuse to the local recycling center.
Please do not leave your refuse on the curb until collection day.
The company will refuse any application submitted after the deadline.
All household refuse must be sorted into the appropriate bins.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) The company will refuse any application submitted after the deadline. The highlighted word refuse is a noun that means "trash," "garbage," or "waste material." The homonym in option (c) is a verb meaning "to decline," "reject," or "say no to." The other options use "refuse" with the same meaning as the highlighted word (trash).

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the underlined word. After experiencing the traumatic event, she struggled with dissociasion.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling of the underlined word is "dissociation". It refers to a mental process where a person disconnects from their thoughts, feelings, memories, or sense of identity. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. The proposal is not a cure for the problem of Britain's economics.
questions.explanation: Amnesty: A pardon, especially for political offences; the granting of forgiveness or immunity to individuals or groups who have committed certain offenses, typically by the government or other authority. Parvenu: A person who has recently or suddenly risen to a higher social class, status, or wealth, especially one who is regarded as being unsophisticated, gauche, or lacking in manners. Haven: A place of safety or refuge; a harbour or port where ships can seek shelter from storms or find protection. Panacea: A remedy or solution for all difficulties or diseases; something that is supposed to cure all ills or solve all problems. So, 'panacea' is the right choice. Hence, option (d) is correct.
